metadata.title: Plan Mode: Freeze Before the Byte Changes

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Jambo, Liam — in for Bear. This is Kia ora, Liam — in for Bear? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: This is Kia ora, Liam — in for Bear?
props.segment: Plan Mode: Freeze Before the Byte

beats.narration_text: Claude Code runs an agentic loop — it gathers, acts, and verifies before reporting back. That loop can read files, edit files, and run terminal commands without pausing. A teacher who pastes a prompt and walks away returns to a classroom website that has been restructured. Not badly. But differently than intended. Shift+Tab twice before that first prompt changes the loop entirely. This is Kia ora, Liam — in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, restructure classroom site

props.command: reorganize the classroom website so the schedule is on the front page

beats.narration_text: Claude Code has three interruption points. Esc stops Claude mid-action — the current operation halts. Plan mode, Shift+Tab twice, puts Claude in read-only: it can read, propose, and explain, but it cannot edit, run, or change anything on disk. Ctrl+G inside plan mode opens the plan in your text editor — you edit it directly, save, and return. Plan mode is the interrupt that lets you see the plan before any file changes.
beats.new_visual_element: Three interrupts — Esc (stop) / Plan mode (freeze writes) / Ctrl+G (edit plan)

beats.narration_text: In plan mode, Claude produces a plan — a list of steps it intends to take. That plan is visible before execution. If a step is wrong — say, it plans to overwrite a file you wanted to keep — you correct the plan, not the aftermath. The correction costs thirty seconds in plan mode. The correction after execution can cost the working content of a file.
beats.new_visual_element: Plan anatomy — visible step list, Ctrl+G edit, before any write
beats.spark_line: Correct the plan, not the aftermath.

beats.narration_text: Ctrl+G opens the plan in your text editor. You see every proposed step as editable text. Remove a step you do not want. Add a constraint. Change a file path. Save. Return to the terminal. The plan is now what you approved — not what Claude proposed. Switch out of plan mode: Claude executes the plan you edited, not the plan it invented.
beats.new_visual_element: Ctrl+G edit loop — proposed plan → teacher edits → approved plan executes

beats.narration_text: Plan mode is cheapest insurance for any operation whose failure would cost more than thirty seconds to recover from. Routine work that touches nothing sensitive does not need it. First sessions do. Destructive operations do. Anything that touches a file you care about does. The cost is thirty seconds to read the plan. The benefit is the avoided rollback and the guarantee that Claude executes the plan the teacher approved.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — when to use plan mode: first session / destructive / care about the file

beats.narration_text: Enter plan mode for your next session. Paste this prompt to practice the edit loop.

props.command: [Switch to plan mode first — Shift+Tab twice] Propose a plan to: [describe a small change to your project]. Do not execute anything. List each step you would take, the file you would touch, and what the change would be. I will review the plan and edit it with Ctrl+G before approving.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated. Plan mode is cheapest insurance for any operation whose failure would cost more than thirty seconds to recover from. Routine work that touches nothing sensitive does not need it. First sessions do. Destructive operations do. Anything that touches a file you care about does. The cost is thirty seconds to read the plan. The benefit is the avoided rollback and the guarantee that Claude executes the plan the teacher approved. Enter plan mode for your next session. Paste this prompt to practice the edit loop.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Plan Mode: Freeze Before the Byte Changes] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
